title: Live field-monitor: estimate mu_field + our rank vs the cut line from the 5-min peer logs (enables B0182 rank-conditional posture)
source: Unlocked by B0158 (mid-round adjustment ALLOWED) + B0182 finalization to Branch A rank-conditional bold-during-cuts.
why: B0182 is rank-conditional: in R1-R3 we escalate/de-escalate the crypto sleeve rung by reading (a) mu_field = field-median per-period equity return + attrition/blow-up rate, and (b) our rank vs the next cut line. PEER-LOG ACCESS IS WEB-ONLY/MANUAL (CEO 2026-06-16) -- no API/feed/export -- so this is a MANUAL-ENTRY DECISION AID, not an auto-ingesting live monitor: the operator transcribes the numbers off the web portal at each touchpoint and the tool maps them to a recommended rung. Without it the posture collapses to the inferior blind pre-commit. Operator playbook: runbook section 6.3.
value_rationale: Directly enables the finalized winning posture (rank-conditional dominates blind). High value, now modest effort: it is a manual-entry decision table + Browne rung policy, NOT an ingestion pipeline -- peer-log access is web-only/manual (CEO 2026-06-16), which REMOVES the original hard dependency (peer-log API format).
scope: PEER-LOG ACCESS RESOLVED (CEO 2026-06-16): leaderboard + peer data are WEB-ONLY/MANUAL -- no API/feed/export. Re-scoped from auto-ingesting monitor to a MANUAL-ENTRY rung decision aid.

Manual read inputs (operator transcribes off the web each touchpoint): our rank, N_active, hours-to-round-end, field-median P&L sign, blow-up/attrition count -> percentile + distance to cut line + sign of mu_field.

Rung-recommendation policy (Browne digital-option, B0182 finalized posture): map (rank vs cut line, hours-left, mu_field sign) -> recommended --lottery-risk-frac on the B0186 ladder, capped at the ~0.06 knee, section-14-safe; largest rung R1, taper R2/R3, flat finals, de-risk if mu_field<0.

Operator playbook lives in runbook section 6.3 (manual cadence ~3-5 touchpoints/round; one MANDATORY end-of-round read ~6-8h before each 22:00 BST boundary; safe flat/minimal launch default between reads; section-14-bounded latches as the unattended safety net).
Build/TDD the policy as a small CLI/sheet against a SYNTHETIC fixture; NO ingestion adapter needed (manual entry). Keep human-in-loop: tool recommends, operator relaunches per runbook section 6.1.
Surface the recommendation + recorded reads in the monitoring dashboard (related B0096).
recommendation: Web-only/manual access (CEO 2026-06-16) REMOVES the original hard dependency (peer-log API format) -- there is no feed to wire. Re-scope from 'live auto-monitor' to a 'manual-entry rung decision aid' + the runbook section 6.3 operator playbook. Keep human-in-loop (tool recommends a rung from hand-entered numbers; operator relaunches per runbook section 6.1). Coarse cadence is fine because the cut is at round-END -- schedule a MANDATORY read ~6-8h before each 22:00 BST boundary; safe flat/minimal defaults + the section-14-bounded latches cover unattended gaps. Build the policy table now against a synthetic fixture (no external dep).
out_of_scope: Auto-executing rung changes without operator confirmation (keep human-in-loop). Finals phase (blinded -> no peer logs -> monitor inactive).

This monitor is the enabler of the whole adaptive edge.

history.reason: Building the manual-entry rung decision aid + rank-conditional sim (CEO: carry on after B0190 finished).

Rank-conditional sim run (scripts/run_rank_conditional_sim.py, 800 trials x 3 regimes; tests/test_rank_conditional_sim.py + test_rung_policy.py = 21 green). FINDING: the manual single-end-of-round rank-conditional policy BEATS flat (P(reach top100) 0.205 vs 0.031, fair) but UNDERPERFORMS static bold_during_cuts on the PRIZE tail by ~10x (P(top25) 0.016 vs 0.198 fair; 0.019 vs 0.205 subfair; 0.006 vs 0.195 toxic). MECHANISM: top-25 is an order-statistic extreme bought by SUSTAINED dispersion (~8%/day); the manual policy injects only ~1.7%/day avg (mean rung R1=0.017) because it stays flat when inside the top-100 cut and escalates only the last 30% of a round -> it clears cuts but rarely builds the tail. It chases the CUT line, not the PRIZE line. Writeup: docs/reviews/2026-06-16-rank-conditional-posture-sim.md. Decision-aid CLI (scripts/rung_advisor.py) + engine (monte_carlo_rank_conditional) verified.
